title: ffi: client_next_event holds Mutex across recv().await — concurrent callers deadlock
description: ffi/src/api/client.rs client_next_event() calls client.event_rx.lock().await.recv().await (line 173). The tokio::sync::Mutex guard is held across the recv().await yield point. If Dart spawns two concurrent Isolates that both call client_next_event() on the same NieClient, the second caller blocks indefinitely on lock().await while the first holds the Mutex waiting for an event. The Dart async* generator pattern documented in the code comment prevents this in normal usage, but FRB does not enforce single-caller access at the type level — any Dart code can call client_next_event() from a second Isolate. The fix is to use tokio::sync::Mutex with a TryLock guard that returns Err immediately if already locked, or to use a futures::channel::mpsc::Receiver directly (which enforces single-owner at the type level).
close_reason: client_next_event now returns error immediately if already polled by another caller
